input: Using the teams-anthropic-integration skill, create a working Teams app integration using Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.5 for message handling. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Also write integration tests that verify the integration compiles and can call the Claude API with a real message. Save everything to the tests/teams-anthropic-integration directory.

Extend the integration with You.com MCP server for web search and content extraction. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Update the integration tests to also verify the MCP integration compiles and can make a live web search query.

Using the ydc-ai-sdk-integration skill, create an application that uses You.com web search with the Vercel AI SDK. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Also write integration tests that verify the integration works and can call the You.com search API with a real query. Save everything to the tests/ydc-ai-sdk-integration directory.
Extend the integration to also support streaming responses. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Update the integration tests to also verify the streaming integration works end-to-end.

Extend the integration to restrict the agent to only the web search tool. Write flat minimal code with no comments or docstrings. Update the integration tests to verify the restricted integration still returns a non-empty response.

Using the ydc-langchain-integration skill, create a LangChain.js application that uses You.com youSearch and youContents tools with a LangChain agent and structured Zod output. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Also write integration tests that verify the agent works and can call the You.com search API with a real query. Save everything to the tests/ydc-langchain-integration-typescript directory.

Extend the integration to also use youContents to extract content from the first search result URL. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Update the integration tests to verify the agent uses both tools and returns a structured response.

Save everything to the tests/ydc-langchain-integration-python directory.

Extend the integration to also use YouSearchTool and YouContentsTool with a LangGraph create_react_agent agent. Write flat minimal code with no comments or docstrings. Update the integration tests to verify the agent uses both tools and returns a non-empty response.

Using the youdotcom-cli skill, write TypeScript integration tests using bun:test that verify all three You.com API endpoints work correctly: Search (GET https://api.you.com/v1/agents/search), Research (POST https://api.you.com/v1/research), and Contents (POST https://ydc-index.io/v1/contents). Use fetch directly — no SDK. Each test should call the real API with a meaningful query, validate the response shape (correct keys exist), and assert on expected content keywords. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Name the test file with a .spec.ts suffix so bun test discovers it. Save everything to the tests/youdotcom-cli directory.

Extend the tests to also cover Search with livecrawl (livecrawl=web&livecrawl_formats=markdown) verifying that .results.web[].contents.markdown is populated, and Research with effort levels (lite and deep) verifying that deeper effort returns longer content. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Update the test file with these additional cases.

Using the youdotcom-api skill, create a working TypeScript application that calls the You.com Research API directly with fetch to get a synthesized, cited answer to a question. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Write integration tests (using .spec.ts suffix) that call the real API and assert on meaningful response content. Save everything to the tests/youdotcom-api-typescript directory.

Extend the integration to also call the You.com Search and Contents APIs to get raw search results and extract full page content from the top result URLs. Write flat minimal code with no comments or TSDoc. Update the integration tests to verify both the Research API and Search + Contents pipelines work with a live query.

Using the youdotcom-api skill, create a working Python application that calls the You.com Research API directly with requests to get a synthesized, cited answer to a question. Write flat minimal code with no comments or docstrings. Write integration tests that call the real API and assert on meaningful response content. Save everything to the tests/youdotcom-api-python directory.

Extend the integration to also call the You.com Search and Contents APIs to get raw search results and extract full page content from the top result URLs.
